parentQuestion: In anatomical position, the ulna is ___ to the radius.

options: Standing upright, facing forward, arms at sides, palms forward
scaffolds.explain: ANATOMICAL POSITION is STANDING UPRIGHT, facing forward, arms at sides, PALMS FORWARD. All anatomical terms reference this position.

wrongExplains.0: Lying face down is prone position; anatomical position is standing.
wrongExplains.2: Sitting isn't anatomical position; it's standing upright.
wrongExplains.3: Lying on one side is lateral recumbent; anatomical position is standing.

scaffolds.explain: MEDIAL means TOWARD THE MIDLINE (center) of the body. The nose is medial to the eyes.

wrongExplains.0: Away from midline is lateral; medial is toward midline.
wrongExplains.2: Above is superior; medial refers to left-right position.
wrongExplains.3: Below is inferior; medial refers to left-right position.

options: Away from the body midline (toward the sides)
scaffolds.explain: LATERAL means AWAY FROM THE MIDLINE (toward the sides). The ears are lateral to the nose.

wrongExplains.0: Toward midline is medial; lateral is away from midline.
wrongExplains.2: Above is superior; lateral refers to side position.
wrongExplains.3: Below is inferior; lateral refers to side position.

options: The forearm bone on the pinky side
scaffolds.explain: The ULNA is the forearm bone on the PINKY (MEDIAL) SIDE. It forms the point of the elbow.

wrongExplains.0: The upper arm bone is the humerus.
wrongExplains.2: Leg bones are tibia and fibula.
wrongExplains.3: Foot bones include tarsals and metatarsals.

options: The forearm bone on the pinky side
The forearm bone on the thumb side
scaffolds.explain: The RADIUS is the forearm bone on the THUMB (LATERAL) SIDE. It rotates during pronation and supination.

wrongExplains.0: The pinky side bone is the ulna.
wrongExplains.2: The upper arm bone is the humerus.
wrongExplains.3: Hand bones are carpals, metacarpals, and phalanges.

scaffolds.q: In anatomical position, which bone is on the thumb side?
scaffolds.explain: The RADIUS is on the THUMB (lateral) side of the forearm. The ulna is on the pinky (medial) side.

wrongExplains.0: The ulna is on the pinky side; radius is on thumb side.
wrongExplains.2: The humerus is the upper arm bone.
wrongExplains.3: The scapula is the shoulder blade.

scaffolds.q: Which side of the forearm is the pinky on?
scaffolds.explain: The pinky is on the MEDIAL SIDE of the forearm (closer to the body's midline in anatomical position).

wrongExplains.0: Lateral is the thumb side; medial is the pinky side.
wrongExplains.2: Superior refers to up/down, not side position.
wrongExplains.3: Inferior refers to up/down, not side position.

scaffolds.q: Is the ulna medial or lateral to the radius?
scaffolds.explain: The ulna is MEDIAL (closer to the midline) to the radius. The ulna is on the pinky side, which is closer to the body's center.

wrongExplains.0: The radius is lateral (thumb side); the ulna is medial (pinky side).
wrongExplains.2: Superior/inferior describe up/down, not side relationships.
wrongExplains.3: The bones are side-by-side, not above/below each other.

scaffolds.q: In anatomical position, the ulna is ___ to the radius?
scaffolds.explain: The ulna is MEDIAL to the radius. The ulna is on the pinky side (closer to midline); the radius is on the thumb side (lateral).

wrongExplains.0: Lateral means farther from midline — that's the radius.
wrongExplains.2: Superior means above; these bones are side-by-side.
wrongExplains.3: Inferior means below; these bones are side-by-side.
